channel.title: Prologue on CSC4200
channel.description: Recent content in Prologue on CSC4200

item.title: Schedule
item.description:
This is a tentative schedule and will change.

Grading details

Assessment Type Weightage Additional Information
In-class Quizzes 20%
Exams (3 exams) 35%
Programming Assignments 35% One late submission allowed. 50% deducted for other late submissions.
Class Participation 10%

Class Schedule

Aug 19 -

item.title: 5200 - Streaming Client Server with ABE
item.description:
Streaming Client-Server with Attribute-Based Encryption (ABE) Assignment

Objective

The primary objective of this assignment is to build a streaming client-server application that employs Attribute-Based Encryption (ABE) for securing key frames. Students are required to implement the client and server, stream media between them, identify key frames to be encrypted, and perform encryption and decryption using ABE. Additionally, the quality of experience (QoE) must be quantified through metrics such as jitter and delay.

item.title: Program 1
item.description:
This is an INDIVIDUAL Assignment (Do not collaborate) Due - Sept 14, Thursday, 11:59PM

Instructions

  1. Create two separate Python files for the server and client, named server.py and client.py, respectively.

item.title: Program 2: Network Packet Handling
item.description:
This is an INDIVIDUAL Assignment (Do not collaborate)

Due - Sept 30, Saturday, 11:59PM

Instructions

Please read the entire instructions and the skeleton code provided for the server and the client before you start coding.

item.title: Program 3 - Simple Protocol
item.description:
This is a GROUP Assignment

Due Oct 21, Submit your work on iLearn.

Objectives

  1. Learn to create network packets.

  2. Learn how packets can be sent over the network.

item.title: Program 4 - Detect a Person
item.description:
Due Date - Nov 20, 2023

Objectives

The objective of this assignment is to design and implement a client-server system using Raspberry Pi devices. The server will control an LED, while the client will sense motion using a Passive Infrared Sensor (PIR) and communicate with the server to blink the LED. The assignment includes establishing a three-way handshake, sending blink duration and count information, acknowledging the data, and responding to motion detection by blinking the LED. The client and server communications MUST use UDP (SOCK_DGRAM) and NOT TCP.
